key_insight: The book reframes persistent homology as a topological, function-based theory of data, emphasizing observers, equivariances, and symmetry-aware analysis rather than only metric-space inputs.
engineering_relevance: It provides a principled bridge from topological data analysis to machine learning via GENEOs, supporting symmetry-invariant and non-expansive model design.
method: Introduces persistent homology from a topological perspective with self-contained background.
Treats data as functions/measurable objects to encode observations and transformations.
Connects TDA to equivariant learning through Group Equivariant Non-Expansive Operators (GENEOs).
evidence_signal: The abstract describes a pedagogical, foundational book rather than an experimental study, so its evidence is based on theoretical exposition and selected standard results rather than benchmark evaluation.
watch_item: The biparameter persistence section on monodromy requires more advanced algebraic topology, which may limit accessibility for some readers or implementers.
paper_title: Persistent Homology and Equivariance in Data Analysis: A Topological Introduction
